title: rudbeckia speciosa var. sullivantii
intro: Rudbeckia speciosa var. sullivantii, also known as Black-Eyed Susan, is a perennial wildflower native to North America. It is a member of the daisy family and has large, bright yellow daisy-like flowers with dark centers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as its ability to lower fever and alleviate cold symptoms. The flowers and roots of Rudbeckia speciosa var. sullivantii can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
